mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, directly increasing equity valuations. A 1-month decline in FEDFUNDS predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days through mechanical repricing of equity risk premiums.
evidence_refs: fact:current FEDFUNDS at 1.55% suggests accommodative stance
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or forward returns following FEDFUNDS declines do not exceed baseline by at least 50 basis points on average (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer purchasing power, triggering equity selloffs as earnings growth expectations decline. A 0.3 percentage point increase in UNRATE over 3 months predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:current UNRATE at 3.6% is historically low
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite rising unemployment, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth (RSAFS) reflects consumer demand strength and corporate revenue expansion. Months with RSAFS growth exceeding the 3-month moving average by > 2% signal robust demand conditions that support equity valuations, predicting positive 20-day returns.
evidence_refs: fact:current RSAFS at 529,766M indicates sustained consumer activity
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with strong consumer demand
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in < 52% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) captures real economic capacity utilization and manufacturing health. Sustained INDPRO growth above trend signals supply-side strength and productivity gains that support corporate profitability, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:current INDPRO at 109.20 shows expansion from 2017 baseline
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns during INDPRO expansion periods do not exceed baseline by at least 100 basis points (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) jointly signal consumer confidence and discretionary spending capacity. When both HOUST and RSAFS accelerate simultaneously (each > 5% month-over-month), the combined demand signal predicts positive 20-day equity returns through multiplier effects on corporate earnings.
evidence_refs: fact:current HOUST at 1,608K and RSAFS at 529,766M
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in < 55% of jointly-triggered instances, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) and inflation (CPIAUCSL) jointly determine real wage dynamics and labor market tightness. When PAYEMS growth accelerates while CPIAUCSL remains stable, real wage gains support consumer spending and equity valuations. This interaction predicts positive 5-day returns.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS > 200K monthly gain AND CPIAUCSL MoM < 0.3%
evidence_refs: fact:current PAYEMS at 152,186K and CPIAUCSL at 258.82
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in < 52% of jointly-triggered instances, or the interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 3.5% and inflation (CPIAUCSL) is rising > 0.4% month-over-month, the Fed is in active tightening mode. This regime-specific condition predicts negative 60-day S&P 500 returns as equity valuations compress under higher discount rates and growth expectations decline.
evidence_refs: fact:current FEDFUNDS at 1.55% is well below tightening threshold
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation acceleration
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window during tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) is the broadest measure of economic expansion. Quarters with GDPC1 growth > 2.5% annualized signal robust aggregate demand and corporate revenue expansion, predicting positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 60 trading days through earnings growth realization.
evidence_refs: fact:current GDPC1 at 19,219.767B indicates sustained economic expansion
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns during high-growth quarters do not exceed low-growth quarters by at least 150 basis points on average (t-test p > 0.10).
